chapter_context.summary: Hezekiah falls mortally ill but is granted fifteen more years and a sign on the sun dial; he foolishly shows the Babylonian envoys his wealth; Isaiah foretells the Babylonian captivity.

outline.text: Hezekiah's Mortal Illness; Prays and is Granted Fifteen More Years
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hezekiah's Mortal Illness; Prays and is Granted Fifteen More Years (20:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: The Sign of the Shadow Moving Backward Ten Degrees on the Dial of Ahaz
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Sign of the Shadow Moving Backward Ten Degrees on the Dial of Ahaz (20:8-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Babylonian Envoys Visit; Hezekiah Shows Them All His Treasures; Isaiah Foretells Exile
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Babylonian Envoys Visit; Hezekiah Shows Them All His Treasures; Isaiah Foretells Exile (20:12-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Hezekiah's Conduit and Water Engineering; Death of Hezekiah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hezekiah's Conduit and Water Engineering; Death of Hezekiah (20:20-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

key_themes.title: Prophetic Authority
key_themes.description: Elisha carries the mantle of Elijah, showing that God's word stands firm in judgment and mercy regardless of political power.

key_themes.title: Covenant Faithfulness and Exile
key_themes.description: The tragic fall of Samaria and Jerusalem shows that persistent rebellion and idolatry inevitably lead to judgment.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 20 prompt you to trust in God's promises even during seasons of spiritual decline?
What warnings or instructions does this chapter hold for guarding your own heart against syncretism?

cultural_historical.title: The Assyrian and Babylonian Empires
cultural_historical.text: The rise of Mesopotamian super-empires (Assyria and Babylon) serves as the historical backdrop for the exile and division of the Hebrew kingdoms.
